topic_title: Writing to a file — open, write, close
verdict: PASS — intent fully covered. Side-by-side contrast of write mode ('w') vs append mode ('a') rendered clearly with color coding: red/pink panel (erase ✗) vs green panel (kept intact ✓). Open → write → auto-close lifecycle visible in both subgraphs. 8 nodes at upper limit — acceptable. LR subgraphs rendered stacked vertically by mmdc rather than truly side-by-side; contrast remains legible. No scope-creep nodes, no bare acronyms, all labels beginner-clear.

node_count.note: 4 nodes per subgraph (open, erase/keep, write methods, auto-close). At upper limit — acceptable for side-by-side contrast layout.

label_clarity.note: open('out.txt', 'w') / open('out.txt', 'a') — file name concrete. 'existing content erased ✗' / 'existing content kept intact ✓' — plain language, checkmark/cross reinforce meaning. '.write() or .writelines()' — method names exact. 'with block → auto-close' — self-explanatory.

intent_coverage.intent: Side-by-side contrast: write mode ('w') erases file then writes from beginning; append mode ('a') adds to end. Both show open → write → auto-close lifecycle.
intent_coverage.note: Both branches present. Erase vs keep distinction is the visual anchor — red node vs green node with explicit text. Lifecycle (open → write → close) readable in each panel.

scope_discipline.note: All 8 nodes strictly within 12.7 scope. No advanced mode nodes (e.g. binary 'rb'/'wb', seek, truncate). No adjacent-topic nodes.

findings.detail: LR flowchart with two subgraphs rendered as vertically stacked panels by mmdc rather than side-by-side. This is a known Mermaid rendering behavior. Contrast is still legible and intent is clear. Not a hard fail.

soft_findings: render_quality: LR subgraphs stacked vertically by mmdc — contrast still clear, not a hard fail
